description: §19.3.3 E-ERROR-009 — `fail` names a FOREIGN enum entirely (`OtherError.Whatever`) when the function's declared error type is `MyError`. Even though Whatever is a valid variant of OtherError, it is not a variant of the DECLARED type, so E-ERROR-009 fires AS an error. Incidental W-DEAD-FUNCTION/W-PROGRAM-001 ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §19.3.3: `fail` SHALL produce a value of the error enum type declared in the function's `!` signature; a variant that does not belong to the declared error type SHALL be a compile error (E-ERROR-009).
